content.full_text:
State of California

Memorandum

To : File 86107 and 86109                                    Date :April 3, 1979


From : FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
       Rich Eichman

Subject: Lobbying Disclosure


It is the staff's opinion that both a lobbyist and a lobbyist's
employer have a reporting obligation when the lobbyist participates
with the employer in the arranging of a gift of less than $10.00 to
a state official even though all costs are paid by the employer.

The lobbyist's employer must report the entire transaction as an
"activity expense."

The lobbyist must submit an attachment to the lobbyist report which
discloses the same information reported on the employer report.
The arrangement is not reported as an "activity expense" by the
lobbyist.

sections.conclusion: It is the staff's opinion that both a lobbyist and a lobbyist's employer have a reporting obligation when the lobbyist participates with the employer in the arranging of a gift of less than $10.00 to a state official even though all costs are paid by the employer. The lobbyist's employer must report the entire transaction as an "activity expense." The lobbyist must submit an attachment to the lobbyist report which discloses the same information reported on the employer report. The arrangement is not reported as an "activity expense" by the lobbyist.
sections.question_synthetic: What are the lobbying disclosure and reporting obligations when a lobbyist and their employer jointly arrange a gift of less than $10 to a state official?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Both the lobbyist's employer and the lobbyist have reporting obligations for gifts under $10 arranged jointly. The employer reports the transaction as an activity expense, while the lobbyist must attach disclosure of the same information but does not report it as an activity expense.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is internal FPPC staff memorandum (not traditional advice letter format); no formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections but substantive legal opinion provided; File numbers 86107 and 86109 referenced; Date is April 3, 1979 but document ID is 79-086.

embedding.summary: FPPC staff memorandum addressing lobbying disclosure obligations when a lobbyist and employer jointly arrange a gift under $10 to a state official. Staff concludes both parties have reporting obligations with specific requirements for each.
